triageFlags.title: Excessive Loan-to-Value (LTV) Ratio
triageFlags.description: The LTV ratio exceeds the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the proposal as the LTV ratio is not within acceptable policy limits.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum LTV for Tier B applicants is 100%. The current LTV of 224.44% is significantly above this threshold, indicating a high risk of financial overextension.

triageFlags.title: Excessive Debt-to-Income (DTI) Ratio
triageFlags.description: The DTI ratio exceeds the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the proposal as the DTI ratio is not within acceptable policy limits.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum DTI of 40% for Tier B applicants. The current DTI of 55% indicates that the applicant's financial obligations are too high relative to their income, posing a significant risk.

content.reasoning: The applicant's LTV ratio of 224.44% and DTI ratio of 55% significantly exceed the maximum allowable thresholds for their credit tier (B). These violations indicate a high financial risk, making the proposal non-compliant with underwriting policies.
